title: Spring Claw Care: Shedding Season Prep
description: As spring progresses into late April, northern hemisphere animals are fully shedding winter coats. Claws need extra attention to remove trapped fur and debris.
main_content: Today's focus is on grooming claws during the seasonal shed. Use a soft bristle brush to gently clean between digits, removing loose fur that can cause matting or irritation. For wild species, this is a natural time for claw sharpening on rough surfaces—provide scratching posts or logs for domestic pets. Check for overgrowth: spring growth spurts can lead to faster nail growth. Trim if needed, but avoid cutting into the quick. A balanced diet rich in biotin and omega-3s supports healthy claw regeneration.
key_takeaways: Brush between claws daily during shedding season to prevent matting.
Provide rough surfaces for natural claw maintenance.
Monitor nail growth; spring can accelerate it.
Nutrition supports claw health—biotin and omega-3s are key.
